new_positions.thesis: Intuitive Surgical dominates robotic-assisted surgery with a near-monopoly on the da Vinci platform. As populations age globally, surgical volume grows and hospitals upgrade to robotic systems for precision and recovery speed. Switching costs are extremely high — once a hospital trains surgeons on da Vinci, they don't switch. RSI at 21 suggests a sharp but likely temporary pullback; MACD remains bullish, meaning the trend is intact. This is a rare opportunity to buy a world-class compounder at a deeply oversold entry point.

lessons: PLTR lesson: When post-earnings analyst downgrades hit a growth stock with already-elevated expectations, RSI and MACD can deteriorate quickly. For smaller/higher-risk positions, consider trimming 25-30% into the first post-earnings breakdown rather than holding full size into the stop — especially when MACD turns bearish without a near-term catalyst to reverse it. ARK buying is a useful sentiment check but not sufficient alone to hold conviction.

Deployment pacing: 76% cash in a bull market where the S&P is hitting ATHs is a performance drag. The cautious approach protects against drawdowns but guarantees underperformance if the bull continues. In the second review, if macro is stable and positions are behaving, deploy at least 20-25% of remaining cash into high-conviction ideas with clean technical setups.

When a thesis depends partly on a single large customer relationship, proactively monitor that customer's vertical integration roadmap — it is a legitimate long-term invalidation risk even if near-term demand from other customers more than compensates.

Deployment urgency in confirmed bull markets: With S&P at ATHs and four clean technical setups available (RSI 44-70, MACD bullish), sitting at 61% cash for a third consecutive review is indefensible. When multiple high-conviction setups exist simultaneously, deploy into all of them in a single review. Do not ration deployments across future reviews when the current review already has the conviction and technical entry signals — opportunity cost compounds weekly.

lessons: Macro shock deployment lesson: when a new COVID variant triggers broad risk-off selling, immediately assess whether portfolio thesis fundamentals are impaired or merely sentiment-affected. Structural tech (AI/cloud/cybersecurity), healthcare, and clean energy theses are not invalidated by a COVID variant — they are often reinforced (e-commerce over in-store, digital payments over cash, healthcare demand elevated). In macro-driven selloffs, prioritize adding to highest-conviction existing positions showing RSI extremes with bullish MACD (ISRG RSI=25 is the signal), then selectively open new positions in quality names being sold indiscriminately. The key filter: is this thesis-broken or macro-panic? If macro-panic in a quality name, it is a deployment trigger, not a wait-and-see signal.

new_positions.thesis: AT&T is a structurally declining legacy telecom executing a value-destroying strategic unwind. The company is spinning off WarnerMedia into a Discovery merger — a deal requiring slashing the dividend, one of the most iconic yield stocks in the market. AT&T carries approximately $180B in total debt following ill-conceived media acquisitions (DirecTV, Time Warner). The core wireless business faces structural competitive pressure from T-Mobile's aggressive pricing and superior post-Sprint network investment. 5G infrastructure requirements demand massive ongoing capex with long and uncertain return timelines. The combination of debt burden, imminent dividend cut, deal execution risk on the WarnerMedia spin-off, and secular wireline decline creates a compelling short thesis. Critically, this is one of the rare cases where the technical breakdown (RSI=38, below SMA50, MACD bearish) reflects genuine fundamental deterioration rather than macro Omicron panic — the macro selloff is masking the stock's independent secular decline.
new_positions.invalidation: AT&T unexpectedly retains full dividend AND wireless subscriber growth meaningfully accelerates AND debt reduction exceeds consensus expectations. Cover immediately if the WarnerMedia deal is cancelled and management commits to dividend preservation with a credible deleveraging plan.

lessons: Broad simultaneous MACD deterioration is a macro signal, not a stock-specific signal: when 30+ tickers simultaneously show bearish MACD, the market is in a broad risk-off event. In these environments, the rare name maintaining bullish MACD (ISRG) stands out as highest-conviction — that divergence from the pack is itself a signal of relative fundamental strength and should trigger concentrated deployment. When adding to positions in a broad bearish-MACD environment, prioritize the exceptions, not the rule.

new_positions.thesis: Novo Nordisk is the world leader in GLP-1 receptor agonists — semaglutide (Ozempic for type 2 diabetes, Wegovy for obesity) represents a paradigm shift in chronic disease management that is directly and structurally aligned with the Aging Populations theme. As global populations age, the prevalence of type 2 diabetes and obesity compounds — NVO is the primary pharmacological beneficiary of this demographic inevitability. GLP-1 drugs are now demonstrating cardiovascular risk reduction beyond glucose control, expanding the addressable patient population significantly and increasing the clinical imperative to prescribe. NVO operates in a high-barrier duopoly with Eli Lilly — branded injectable switching costs are high once patients are stabilised on a regimen, and the manufacturing complexity of peptide biologics creates meaningful scale moats. Double-digit earnings growth, strong free cash flow, and a dividend growth track record provide defensive characteristics that balance our more volatile tech positions. ASH 2021 conference data this week is confirming the Aging Populations thesis broadly — REGN in myeloma, LLY in oncology, and the broader biotech sector is showing R&D productivity that validates staying long quality healthcare compounders. NVO at RSI=68, MACD bullish, above SMA50 is a technically constructive entry in a confirmed uptrend — not chasing overbought levels but participating in healthy momentum.

lessons: Supply challenges in pharmaceutical positions: when a key drug faces supply constraints (as with NVO/Wegovy), distinguish between demand-driven supply shortfalls (bullish long-term — demand exceeds capacity) and manufacturing/quality-driven supply failures (bearish — process problems). NVO's Wegovy supply issue appears demand-driven, which preserves the thesis but creates a 1-4 quarter revenue timing headwind. Rule: do not add to a pharmaceutical position in the same week it announces supply constraints AND receives an analyst downgrade — wait for FY22 guidance clarity before sizing up. The concurrent downgrade and supply announcement in the same week is a signal to pause, not sell.

Short position MACD divergence as a cover warning: when a SHORT position's underlying stock develops bullish MACD while fundamental thesis remains intact, it signals elevated short-squeeze risk even if the stop has not been triggered. Protocol: when a short position develops bullish MACD, tighten mental stop to within $1-1.50 of current price (rather than waiting for the full hard stop) and require a fresh negative catalyst to hold conviction. The fundamental thesis being intact is necessary but not sufficient — a bullish MACD on a short means the market is disagreeing with us in the near term and we should reduce, not hold full size through technical adversity.

new_positions.thesis: AT&T re-short at materially better prices and a more overbought technical setup. The $180B debt burden has not changed. The structural wireline decline has not changed. T-Mobile's competitive pressure has not changed. The irony is that value/income rotation into T has made the short re-entry cleaner — RSI=76 is the most overbought T has been in years, and it is trading at $27.28 vs our stopped-out entry of $23.28. We are re-shorting a structurally deteriorating business at a 17% higher price into extreme technical overbought conditions, with a known fundamental catalyst (dividend cut) on an approaching timeline. This is a better short entry than the original.

theme_updates.name: AI/Automation
theme_updates.reason: Microsoft Q2 cloud revenue of $22.1B validates the AI/cloud infrastructure thesis — but macro rate shock is compressing multiples regardless of fundamentals. Thesis conviction unchanged, execution environment temporarily adverse.

lessons: Rate-shock selloffs differ fundamentally from COVID-panic selloffs and require a different deployment protocol. In COVID panics, quality names maintain bullish MACD (fear is temporary, thesis intact), and RSI extremes below 35 with bullish MACD are clean buying opportunities. In rate-shock selloffs (Fed signals aggressive tightening, growth-to-energy rotation, ALL growth names showing bearish MACD simultaneously), MACD turns bearish broadly because the repricing is fundamental — higher discount rates structurally compress growth multiples, not temporarily. Trigger for this distinction: when 30+ tickers simultaneously show bearish MACD AND energy/commodity names are the only bullish MACD exceptions, the market is in rate-shock mode, not panic mode. In this environment: (1) do NOT deploy into RSI-extreme longs even in highest-conviction names — RSI extreme alone is insufficient when the repricing driver is structural, (2) wait for MACD to begin stabilising or turning before adding new longs, (3) the dual confirmation signal changes to: RSI extreme PLUS MACD turning/flattening, not RSI alone. Rate repricing can persist 4-8 weeks longer than COVID-variant selloffs.

lessons: Lesson 21 — RSI extreme with MACD bearish during confirmed fundamental deterioration is a falling knife, not a re-entry: META at RSI=28 MACD bearish coincides with the first-ever DAU decline and deeply negative Q1 guidance. This is NOT a macro-panic oversold entry — it is a business going through genuine fundamental stress (user stagnation, metaverse cash burn, rising costs, weakening ad pricing). The filter: was the selloff caused by macro-panic (thesis intact, temporary) or confirmed fundamental deterioration (first-ever metric reversal, guidance miss, structural cost escalation)? When RSI extreme coincides with a confirmed fundamental break — first-ever negative user metric, management guidance well below consensus — the oversold reading is a warning that prior valuation assumptions are being permanently repriced, not a contrarian buy signal. Protocol: only apply RSI-extreme re-entry logic when the thesis is intact; when the metric that defines the thesis (MAU growth, revenue growth rate) shows its first structural reversal, treat RSI extreme as a falling knife indicator regardless of how low it reads.
